records.ety: [L. conflatus, p. p. of conflare to blow together; con- + flare to blow.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Conflated; p. pr. & vb. n. Conflating.]

defs: To blow together; to bring together; to collect; to fuse together; to join or weld; to consolidate.
quotes: The State-General, created and conflated by the passionate effort of the whole nation.

defs: to ignore distinctions between, by treating two or more distinguishable objects or ideas as one; to confuse.
